Fnrn Polrrrcnl PRACTTcEs Courr,rrssroN
.128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95gl.l-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (9t6) 322-0886
April23,2009
Marjorie Evangelista
3437 Cannon Way
Live Oak, CA 95953
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-093
Dear Ms. Evangelista:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act";r and serves to notify you that we have
withdrawn your request per our telephone conversation on April 21, 2009. It is the
Commission's policy not to answer purely hypothetical questions, or questions where the facts
presented are insufficient or too vague to render specific advice. (Regulation 18329(bX2XB),
(8XC) and (c)(a)(D), (F).)
We have enclosed an informational booklet entitled "Can I Vote?" which gives an
overview of the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions. This booklet may assist you i1 determining
whether you have a conflict-of-interest issue in the future.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Commission Counsel
BGLjgl
Enclosure
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through gl0l4. All statutory
ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fiir political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
qi?,

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor submitted a request for advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but the specific question details are not provided in this withdrawal letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request per telephone conversation on April 21, 2009, citing the Commission's policy against answering purely hypothetical questions or questions with insufficient or vague facts. The FPPC provided an informational booklet on conflict-of-interest provisions instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Document references telephone conversation as basis for withdrawal. Regulatory citations provided for FPPC's policy rationale.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor submitted a request for advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but the specific question details are not provided in this withdrawal letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request per telephone conversation on April 21, 2009, citing the Commission's policy against answering purely hypothetical questions or questions with insufficient or vague facts. The FPPC provided an informational booklet on conflict-of-interest provisions instead.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter from the FPPC declining to provide advice on a conflict-of-interest question submitted by Marjorie Evangelista, as the request contained hypothetical or insufficiently detailed facts. The FPPC provided educational materials as an alternative resource.
